frameworks.name: abstract emotion representation
frameworks.one_line: Emotions encoded independently of sensory modality of the eliciting stimulus.

frameworks.name: multivoxel pattern analysis (searchlight MVPA)
frameworks.one_line: Cross-modal classifier decodes emotion across stimulus and memory cues.

frameworks.name: associative learning of emotion-neutral pairings
frameworks.one_line: Memory of an emotional event reactivates abstract emotion representation.

core_question: Do abstract emotion representations in the brain reactivate when triggered by memory cues, not just direct sensory input?
key_assumptions: Emotions can be represented at a sensory-independent abstract level.
Successful associative learning links neutral fractals to specific emotions.
Cross-decoding indicates shared neural code between stimulus and memory.
manipulations: Stimulus type: emotional videos (faces vs bodies) vs fractal cues.
Train/test direction: classifier trained on emotional stimuli tested on fractals (and vice versa).
key_findings: Cross-modal emotion decoding succeeded in PCC, precuneus, MPFC, and angular gyrus.
Stimulus-only emotion decoding yielded clusters in PCC, precuneus, and MPFC.
Multidimensional scaling showed clustering of activation patterns by emotion across stimulus types.
interpretations: Abstract emotion representations are activated by both direct stimuli and learned associations.
PCC, precuneus, MPFC, and angular gyrus support modality-independent emotion codes.
